$comment: Normatively defined by the AIP Registry Interface Agent Revocation Status Endpoint. Response body for GET /v1/agents/{aid}/revocation. The response is a status snapshot, not a signed revocation artifact; active_revocations contains the signed Revocation Objects that support the status.
title: AIP Revocation Status Response
description: Registry response for live revocation status lookups. Returned with HTTP 200 for every registered AID, including AIDs with no active revocations. Unknown AIDs return unknown_aid (HTTP 404) and do not use this schema.

aid.description: The registered AID whose revocation status was checked. MUST equal the path {aid} after percent-decoding.

checked_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which the authoritative Registry evaluated revocation status.

status.description: active: no active revocations apply. restricted: one or more scope_revoke or delegation_revoke records apply, but no full_revoke or principal_revoke applies. revoked: a full_revoke applies to this AID, a principal_revoke targets this AID, or a principal_revoke targets the root Principal DID associated with this AID; Credential Tokens for this AID MUST be rejected.

revoked.description: True when a full_revoke applies to this AID, a principal_revoke targets this AID, or a principal_revoke targets the root Principal DID associated with this AID. Relying Parties MUST reject Credential Tokens for this AID when true.

delegation_revoked.description: True when an active delegation_revoke applies to this AID. Relying Parties MUST reject delegation-chain use rooted at this AID when true; direct principal authorisations for the AID may remain valid unless revoked is also true.

scopes_revoked.description: Union of active scope_revoke scopes for this AID. Empty when no active scope revocation applies.

active_revocations.description: Signed Revocation Objects that currently affect this AID. Empty when status is active. If the AID is affected by a principal-wide principal_revoke, this array includes the signed Revocation Object whose target_id is the Principal DID.
